figures.caption: Figure 1: GSM8K accuracy across model scales. Dashed gray: base model. Error bars: ±1σ where multi-seed data is available. The V-shaped trajectories from 3B to 7B visualize the scale-driven ranking inversion; DPO’s error bar at 3B reflects genuine seed variance (σ = 2.01 pp). At 1.5B, SGRPO achieves the highest accuracy (58.0%). Note: 0.5B–3B use full FT; 7B uses LoRA.
Table 11: Compute budget breakdown on H100 80GB GPUs.

Table 9: Strict-match vs. flexible-extract accuracy on GSM8K. 3B full FT values are from original single runs (pre-seed-fix). At 7B, SimPO has the best format compliance (+4.9 pp) while SFT has the worst (−3.9 pp).

Table 10: General-domain evaluation at 7B (LoRA): ARC-Challenge (acc_norm), HellaSwag (acc_norm), WinoGrande (acc). Despite a 9.4 pp GSM8K spread at 7B, the general-domain spread is just 0.71 pp. Rankings bear no resemblance to GSM8K rankings.

Table 3: GSM8K exact-match accuracy (%) across scales. 1.5B values are seed-fix means with ±σ across 3 seeds (Appendix G). 3B uses full FT with seed-fixed reruns; DPO reports mean ±σ over 2 seeds, ‡single seed available. LoRA results at 3B appear in Table 2. Best per scale in bold. †Epoch-1 checkpoint. ∗Pathological failure. §62.9% with flexible extraction but 10.69% strict-match, indicating format non-compliance. CISPO = Clipped Importance-Sampled Policy Optimization, a sentence-level GRPO variant with per-token ratio clipping.
Figure 4: Training determinism at 0.5B: per-seed GSM8K accuracy for each algorithm across 3 seeds. Five of six algorithms produce identical accuracy (σ = 0). Only IPO shows variance (σ = 0.27).

Figure 3: Training dynamics comparison at 0.5B scale. Left: training loss over steps (smoothed). Right: validation loss over epochs. DPO converges to lower loss; SimPO maintains higher, noisier loss with flat validation loss.
Table 7: Shared training configuration across all experiments.

Table 5: General-domain evaluation at 1.5B: ARC-Challenge (acc_norm), HellaSwag (acc_norm), WinoGrande (acc). All models trained on GSM8K. The 19.3 pp GSM8K spread collapses to 0.47 pp on general reasoning (41× compression). No method meaningfully deviates from the base model.

Figure 2: GSM8K accuracy across 5 seeds for 20 DPO variants at 1.5B (100 runs total). Dashed line: vanilla DPO mean (49.76%). Error bars: ±1 std. †SimPO is the only variant significantly different from DPO (p < 0.0026 after Bonferroni correction)—and it is worse.
Table 1: Taxonomy of 20 DPO variants evaluated in this study, organized by primary modification to the DPO objective (Eq. 1). All evaluated at 1.5B on GSM8K with 5 seeds.

Table 6: Full DPO variant results at 1.5B scale on GSM8K (5 seeds). ∆ is the accuracy difference from vanilla DPO. † indicates statistical significance after Bonferroni correction (p < 0.0026). Cohen’s d measures practical effect size.
Table 13: Compute-performance Pareto frontier. GPU-h/pp = GPU-hours per percentage point gained over base. At 1.5B, SGRPO achieves highest accuracy at 10× SFT’s cost per point.
